title: NOC 85111 – Oil and gas drilling, servicing and related labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 85111 professionals, commonly known as "Floorhands" or "Roughnecks," are the essential physical force behind Canada’s energy production. As a TEER 5 occupation, these labourers perform a variety of manual and support tasks on drilling and service rigs, both on land and offshore. In 2026, as Canada integrates "Clean-Tech" drilling and carbon-capture infrastructure, these workers are in high demand to maintain the operational pace of the sector. For immigration, this role is a premier entry point through the Alberta Opportunity Stream and the New 2026 TEER 5 PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level5Major Group85 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersSub-Major Group851 – Harvesting, landscaping and natural resources labourersMinor Group8511 – Mine and oil and gas drilling, services and related labourersNOC Code85111

duties: Rig Floor Support: Assist in setting up and taking down drilling and service rigs; handle and stack drill pipes, casing, and other equipment on the rig floor.
Maintenance: Clean and maintain the rig floor, tools, and equipment; assist in the maintenance of mechanical systems under the direction of the Motorhand.
Material Handling: Load and unload supplies and equipment from trucks or supply vessels; mix drilling mud and chemicals as required.
Operational Assistance: Assist in connecting and disconnecting pipe sections during "tripping" operations (moving pipe in and out of the wellbore).
Safety: Participate in safety meetings and emergency drills; ensure the work area is free of hazards and spills.
employment_requirements: No formal education is strictly required, though secondary school completion is preferred.
On-the-Job Training: Employers provide 1–3 weeks of intensive site-specific training.

Certifications (Mandatory):H2S Alive (Hydrogen Sulphide Awareness).Standard First Aid & CPR.WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System).Common Safety Orientation (CSO) via Energy Safety Canada.
Common Safety Orientation (CSO) via Energy Safety Canada.
Physical stamina, the ability to lift 45kg+, and a clean drug/alcohol test are essential for site access.
salary_prospects: Note: Annual earnings often reach $75,000 - $95,000 for entry-level floorhands when accounting for overtime and remote site premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. With Canada's natural gas exports reaching record highs and new carbon-capture projects breaking ground, the need for rig support staff is projected to grow by 12% by 2028. The industry's high turnover rate (due to promotions and the demanding nature of the work) ensures a continuous stream of openings for new international applicants.
labour_market_demand: The demand for oil and gas labourers is Very Good, particularly in the Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin. In 2026, Alberta (Fort McMurray/Grand Prairie) and Saskatchewan are reporting critical labor shortages as drilling activity for both traditional energy and lithium extraction intensifies. Because the work is physically demanding and often remote, there is a constant cycle of recruitment for international workers who are ready to build a career in the "Oil Patch."
Highest TEER 5 Pay: Oil and gas labor is among the highest-paid entry-level work in Canada, with starting wages often double those of retail or hospitality.
Rapid Promotion Path: Most Floorhands promote to Motorhand (NOC 84101) or Derrickhand within 12–18 months of consistent performance.
FIFO Lifestyle: Benefit from Fly-In-Fly-Out or "Camp" schedules, where your food, travel, and accommodation are fully covered by the employer.
World-Class Safety: Gain experience in a sector that utilizes the most advanced safety protocols and automated equipment in the global energy industry.
pathways: New 2026 TEER 4 & 5 Pathway: A federal stream targeting essential workers with 12 months of Canadian work experience.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A high-conversion route for those currently working in Alberta’s energy sector.
Saskatchewan INP (Semi-Skilled Natural Resources): Targeted at workers with at least 6 months of experience in the province.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): Accessible for offshore roustabouts with a job offer from designated employers in Newfoundland & Labrador.
